extra.reason: Implemented a reusable shell-owned SymbolSearch component wired to /v1/subjects/resolve and reused by the top bar plus watchlist rail.
Added the P1.1-compatible quote snapshot stub with listing-oriented shape including as_of, delay_class, currency, source_id, and recent range data.
Rendered the subject-detail landing with canonical display identity, listing context, price, absolute and percentage move, freshness/session state, and a small recent-range chart.
Implemented debounced symbol typeahead with ranked inline candidates, arrow-key highlight movement, explicit-choice hydration, and Enter auto-advance for unique results.

Completed all P0.4 child work: shared symbol search/typeahead, resolver handoff into symbol detail, P1.1 quote stub, and first quote snapshot landing. Verified with web tests, typecheck, lint, and production build.

Added quoteFromSubjectRef in web/src/symbol/quote.ts that routes watchlist row hydration through the same createQuoteSnapshotStub that subject detail uses. Factored subjectFromRef out of subjectFromRouteParam in search.ts so the URL-entered landing path and the watchlist row path build the minimal ResolvedSubject the exact same way. ManualWatchlist.tsx now renders each row as a Link(to=symbolDetailPathForSubject) with primary ticker/kind label, secondary MIC·currency, price (formatQuotePrice), signed percent move (direction-colored), and a freshness title attribute; the Remove button is a sibling outside the Link for accessibility. WatchlistSlot.tsx comment updated. Verification: new quote.test.ts equivalence test asserts deepEqual(quoteFromSubjectRef(ref), createQuoteSnapshotStub(subjectFromRouteParam(subjectRouteParam(ref)))) — row and landing share one derivation for the same subject. Live-verified in browser: row listing:1111… showed $108.71 / -1.62%, landing showed $108.71 / -1.79 (-1.62%); issuer:3333… row showed $239.91 / +0.17%, landing showed $239.91 / +0.41 (+0.17%). All 43 web tests pass, typecheck + lint + vite build green.

Replaced the SaveToWatchlistProtectedAction.payload from {symbol: string} to {subject_ref: SubjectRef, display_name?: string} since the watchlist persists canonical SubjectRefs. SubjectDetailShell now renders a Save-to-watchlist button below the QuoteSnapshot in the public subject-detail header; click fires requestProtectedAction with the resolved subject_ref + display_name. WatchlistSlot registers a useResumedProtectedAction handler for SaveToWatchlist that calls watchlist.addSubject(action.payload.subject_ref) — when an unauth click queues the pending action and the user signs in, AuthInterruptProvider dispatches the resume and the membership add lands automatically. authInterruptState.isProtectedAction validates the subject_ref shape (kind ∈ SUBJECT_KINDS, non-empty id) plus optional string display_name. Retired the HomePage P0.1.3 scaffold (Save AAPL button) — replaced by the real CTA on subject detail per its own deprecation comment. Verification: 45/45 web tests pass (including new round-trip and rejection tests for the new payload shape); typecheck + lint + vite build green; live E2E in browser exercised four flows on /symbol/listing:1111…/overview: (1) unauth click opens modal with 'Saving Listing subject…' message and stores pending action with subject_ref+returnTo; (2) Sign in dispatches and adds to watchlist while preserving the URL; (3) authed click adds immediately without modal; (4) Cancel dismisses modal, clears pending, preserves URL, does not add.

Implemented MarketDataAdapter interface (services/market/src/adapter.ts) with NormalizedQuote/NormalizedBars carrying spec §6.2.1 metadata (as_of, delay_class, currency, source_id, adjustment_basis); first adapter is Polygon (services/market/src/adapters/polygon.ts) with injected fetcher + listing-context resolver. 9 tests pass including the bead's verification clause: a fixture adapter and the Polygon adapter produce identical consumer output. Sibling subtasks fra-cw0.1.2/.1.3/.1.4/.1.5/.1.6 will tighten the quote/bar contracts, availability outcomes, corp actions, and dev-api wiring.

Implemented quote contract via smart constructor + schema assertion (services/market/src/quote.ts). Spec §6.2.1 metadata fields (as_of, delay_class, currency, source_id) plus parent-epic move math (prev_close, change_abs, change_pct) are all validated at construction; assertQuoteContract re-asserts the same invariants for any adapter. Polygon adapter migrated from /v2/last/trade to /v2/snapshot to source prev_close in one round trip. New contract.test.ts proves: (a) Polygon adapter satisfies the contract, (b) fixture adapter satisfies the contract, (c) a deliberately-broken adapter is correctly rejected. 20 new tests, all 29 in services/market pass.

Implemented bar contract via smart constructor + schema assertion (services/market/src/bar.ts) mirroring the quote-contract pattern from fra-cw0.1.2. NormalizedBars carries spec §6.2.1 metadata (listing, interval, range, as_of, delay_class, currency, source_id, adjustment_basis); the constructor and assertBarsContract enforce: required-field presence, ISO 4217 currency, UUID v4 source_id, ISO-8601 timestamps with explicit offset, BarInterval/AdjustmentBasis enums, range start < end, every bar's ts in [start, end), strictly ascending ts, OHLC invariants (high>=low, high>=max(open,close), low<=min(open,close)), and volume>=0. Extracted shared validators to services/market/src/validators.ts (used by both quote.ts and bar.ts). Polygon getBars now routes through normalizedBars(). Bead verification clause: contract.test.ts asserts the bars contract for Polygon (adjusted=true), Polygon (adjusted=false), and a fixture adapter; a deliberately-broken adapter is correctly rejected. 21 new tests, all 49 in services/market pass.

Implemented MarketDataOutcome envelope (available/unavailable). Polygon adapter classifies provider errors (PolygonFetchError 5xx -> provider_error retryable, 404 -> missing_coverage, 429 -> rate_limited, malformed payload -> provider_error not_retryable). Pre-fetch caller-side range validation continues to throw. Verification clause covered: contract test simulates 503 -> caller sees normalized envelope with no raw provider fields. 78 tests pass.

Added CorporateAction union (Split | CashDividend | StockDividend | SpinOff) with effective_date and source_id, smart constructor with per-kind validation, and applyCorporateActions(bars, actions, basis) that walks raw bars to a target AdjustmentBasis. Splits + stock dividends adjust price + volume; cash dividends + spin-offs scale pre-ex prices via the prevClose factor leaving volume untouched. Oldest-first action ordering composes correctly (verified across compounded splits and split+dividend mixes). Verification clause covered: 16 dedicated tests, including continuity-across-split-date (max day-to-day jump <= 1 unit after adjustment, vs >= 50 unit drop in unadjusted). 73 tests pass total.

Wired up market HTTP service. Backend: services/market/src/http.ts exposes GET /v1/market/quote that goes through the polygon adapter seam, with a dev-mode fixture fetcher (services/market/src/dev-fixtures.ts) and an in-memory listing repo (services/market/src/listings.ts). Real source_id (DEV_POLYGON_SOURCE_ID UUID) flows through every response. Frontend: removed createQuoteSnapshotStub and quoteFromSubjectRef; added fetchQuoteSnapshot async fetcher; QuoteSnapshot.tsx and ManualWatchlist row both fetch via useEffect with loading/unavailable states. QuoteSnapshot type aligned with the spec contract (delay_class names, session_state names) — dropped stub-only fields (display_name, recent_range, issuer_profile) and let the component access them from ResolvedSubject directly. Vite proxy adds /v1/market route. Dev shell runs the market service on port 4321. Verification: smoke test curls source_id=00000000-0000-4000-a000-000000000001 (live UUID, not p1.1-stub). 64 market tests + 47 web tests + typecheck all green.

All three children closed: fra-6al.6.1 (default manual watchlist + membership CRUD), fra-6al.6.2 (row quote hydration via shared listing-oriented snapshot), fra-6al.6.3 (add-to-watchlist with inline auth interrupt). Note: row quote hydration was migrated from the P0.4 stub to the real market service in fra-cw0.1.6 (each row now fetches via /v1/market/quote).

Closed
Closed

Series query binding implemented with smart-constructor + assertContract pattern. All 5 dimensions (subject_refs, range, interval, basis, normalization) explicitly bound and rejection on missing dimension verified. 131 market tests pass.
Implemented canonical series cache identity and key verification
Implemented snapshot transform reuse gate with allowed range/interval pairs, identity preservation, and as_of freshness checks
Implemented explicit per-subject series coverage windows and unavailable history flags; verified with services/market npm test.

Implemented key-stats aggregation for margins, revenue growth, and P/E with explicit basis/period/as_of assumptions, visible input refs, unavailable/warning states, and source-backed price input handling; verified with services/fundamentals npm test.

Landed: POST /v1/market/series HTTP handler in services/market/src/http.ts consumes NormalizedSeriesQuery, fans out per-listing via adapter.getBars, returns per-listing MarketDataOutcome envelopes inside a 200. Per the contract: rejects non-raw normalization at the request layer (400, transforms ship later), reclassifies basis-mismatched bars as missing_coverage (never silently relabels), surfaces unknown listings as missing_coverage rather than provider_error. dev-fixtures.ts extended with deterministic synthetic-bar generation so dev mode serves both /quote and /series. 13 new HTTP tests + smoke-tested live: 144 pass, 0 fail.

Landed: services/fundamentals/src/http.ts exposes GET /v1/fundamentals/profile. Added IssuerProfile envelope (subject + legal_name + former_names + cik + lei + domicile + sector + industry + exchanges with canonical listing SubjectRefs + as_of + source_id), an IssuerProfileRepository abstraction with InMemory backing, and a fundamentals-flavored availability envelope mirroring services/market's pattern (subject-anchored on issuer rather than listing). Dev mode (port 4322) seeds 5 issuer fixtures with listing UUIDs aligned to services/market dev-fixtures so overview tab can chain profile→quote. 28 new tests (17 contract + 11 HTTP integration), all green; 103 pass, 0 fail. Smoke-tested: known issuer 200, unknown 404 with structured envelope, malformed inputs 404, POST 404, repo failure 502.

Landed: GET /v1/fundamentals/stats handler in services/fundamentals/src/http.ts wraps buildKeyStats() and returns the derivation-transparent KeyStatsEnvelope. Added StatsRepository abstraction (mirror of IssuerProfileRepository) returning BuildKeyStatsInput bundles, and DEV_STATS_INPUTS fixtures: AAPL with full inputs (current+prior+price → 5 stats compute) and NVDA with sparse inputs (current only → margins compute, revenue_growth_yoy + pe_ratio carry missing_* warnings unmodified). Cherry-picked the key-stats aggregator commit (PR #21) as a separate commit so it drops out cleanly when PR #21 lands. 10 new HTTP tests verify: full envelope materializes, basis/period/as_of present on every stat, per-input source_id reaches the wire (provenance), sparse warnings flow through, unknown issuer 404 with structured envelope, 502 on repo throw. 121 pass, 0 fail. Smoke-tested live: AAPL 200, NVDA 200, unknown 404.

Landed: web/src/pages/symbol/OverviewSection.tsx composes the symbol-detail overview tab. Profile card (legal_name, sector, industry, domicile, CIK, LEI, exchanges) reads /v1/fundamentals/profile via fetchIssuerProfile. Key stats row (5 stats with format-aware values + warning indicators) reads /v1/fundamentals/stats via fetchKeyStats. Performance card renders a 30-day SVG sparkline from /v1/market/series via fetchSeries. Subject is passed to children via Outlet context (new useSubjectDetailContext hook in shell/subjectDetailOutletContext.ts). Bare-URL entry degrades gracefully: perf chart still renders (uses listing UUID); profile + stats show 'Issuer context unavailable — open from search' message. Hydrated entry (location.state.subject with context.issuer) renders all four sections fully. Smoke-tested live in browser with both paths. Vite proxy extended to /v1/fundamentals (port 4322). 20 new fetch-helper tests + existing 48 = 68 web tests pass; typecheck and lint clean. Old sections.tsx OverviewSection placeholder split into its own module per the long-standing TODO.

Landed: POST /v1/fundamentals/statements handler in services/fundamentals/src/http.ts. Validates issuer SubjectRef + statement family + periods array (parsed via parsePeriod with strict {year}-{FY|Q1..Q4} format) + basis enum. Returns per-period results with the binding query echoed; missing-period misses surface as missing_coverage envelopes per-period (siblings unaffected). StatementRepository abstraction mirrors IssuerProfileRepository / StatsRepository; pre-validates via normalizedStatement at construction. dev-statement-fixtures.ts ships 5 years of AAPL income (FY2020-FY2024 as_reported) plus FY2020 as_restated to exercise the basis branch. 14 new HTTP tests verify: full multi-period materializes, per-period misses isolated, basis branch plumbed to per-line data, malformed period 400, duplicate period 400, empty array 400, non-issuer subject_ref 400, unknown family 400, malformed JSON 400, wrong content-type 415, GET 404, repo-throws 502. 135 pass, 0 fail. Smoke-tested live: AAPL 2024+2023 200, unknown period 200 with structured per-period miss, malformed period 400.

Landed: POST /v1/fundamentals/segments handler in services/fundamentals/src/http.ts. Validates issuer SubjectRef + axis enum (business/geography) + period (parsePeriod) + basis enum. Returns single SegmentFactsEnvelope per (axis, period) request, or 404 missing_coverage when unavailable. SegmentsRepository abstraction mirrors StatsRepository: pre-runs buildSegmentFacts at construction, hot-path is a Map.get keyed on issuer+axis+basis+period. dev-segment-fixtures.ts ships Apple's real FY2024 + FY2023 business breakdowns (iPhone, Mac, iPad, Wearables, Services) plus FY2024 geography (Americas, Europe, Greater China, Japan, Rest of Asia Pacific), each with consolidated revenue totals so the aggregator's reconciliation path is exercised. 14 new HTTP tests verify: full envelope materializes, axis branch plumbed, coverage_warnings array reaches the wire (zero on consistent fixture), 404 with structured envelope for missing axis/period combo, 404 for unknown issuer, malformed period 400, unknown axis 400, non-issuer subject_ref 400, unknown basis 400, malformed JSON 400, wrong content-type 415, GET 404, repo-throws 502, segment_definitions axis labels preserved. Cherry-picked the segment-facts aggregator (PR #22, 4 commits squashed to latest state at 0847912) so this branch can wire HTTP without waiting for the PR to merge — drops out cleanly on rebase. 171 pass, 0 fail. Smoke-tested live: business 200, geography 200, missing period 404 with structured envelope.

Landed: web/src/pages/symbol/FinancialsSection.tsx composes the three pieces the bead's verification clause names: (a) income statement table for the last 5 fiscal years (revenue, COGS, gross profit, opex, operating income, net income, EPS basic+diluted), (b) basis toggle (as_reported vs as_restated), (c) segment donut + per-segment revenue trajectory list with axis toggle (business vs geography). Per-period misses become em-dash cells so the basis toggle reveals 'this period wasn't restated' without collapsing the table. Extracted useFetched + VisibleFetchState into web/src/symbol/useFetched.ts so OverviewSection and FinancialsSection share the same key-tracked stale-protection pattern. New fetch helpers: web/src/symbol/statements.ts (POST /v1/fundamentals/statements + recentFyPeriods + findLineValue) and web/src/symbol/segments.ts (POST /v1/fundamentals/segments + sumSegmentMetric + axisLabel). 10 new fetch-helper tests + existing 67 = 77 web tests pass; typecheck and lint clean (refactored mutable-offset SVG render to an immutable reduce per React 19 lint rule). Smoke-tested live in browser: AAPL FY2020-FY2024 income with real values, basis toggle reveals FY2020 restated EPS (3.30 vs 3.31), segment donut shows iPhone 51.4% / Services 24.6% on business axis and Americas 42.7% / Europe 25.9% / Greater China 17.1% on geography axis (real AAPL FY2024 breakdown).

Landed: web/src/pages/symbol/EarningsSection.tsx composes the three pieces the bead's verification clause names: (a) earnings chronology table for last 8 quarters (estimate/actual/surprise % with green ▲ for beats, red ▼ for misses), (b) analyst consensus rating distribution bar (5 buckets) plus per-period forward EPS+revenue estimates, (c) price target range (low/mean/median/high with mean+median markers on a positional bar). Two new fetch helpers: web/src/symbol/consensus.ts (GET /v1/fundamentals/consensus + ratingLabel) and web/src/symbol/earnings.ts (GET /v1/fundamentals/earnings). 7 new helper tests + existing 76 = 83 web tests pass; typecheck and lint clean. Smoke-tested live in browser: AAPL FY2023 Q1 - FY2024 Q4 earnings table renders with real EPS surprise %, consensus shows 14 strong_buy / 17 buy / 8 hold / 1+1 sell, target -280 (mean $220.50). Overview tab regression-clean (Apple profile + key stats + 30d sparkline still render).
Closed

Holders tab implemented end-to-end. Backend: HoldersEnvelope (institutional + insider) + repository + GET /v1/fundamentals/holders?kind=. Frontend: holders.ts fetcher + HoldersSection.tsx with two tables. Verified with 30 new tests (241 fundamentals, 89 web, all green) and live in-browser AAPL render via Playwright (Vanguard/BlackRock/Berkshire institutional + Cook/Maestri insider transactions).

Signals tab implemented as evidence-backed surface (sentiment_trend + claim_clusters blocks). Vendor-agnostic naming preserved (no /reddit, no /news routes). Frontend fixture loader returns deterministic per-issuer data shaped like the BaseBlock contract (id, snapshot_id, data_ref, source_refs, as_of) so swapping in real P3/P4.6 wiring stays mechanical. 10 new tests; 99 web tests green, typecheck + lint + build clean. Verified live in browser for AAPL (5 clusters across bullish/bearish/neutral with mixed community/news/filing evidence) and NVDA (distinct sentiment +0.23 vs +0.18, distinct cluster ordering).

Analyze entry handoff implemented end-to-end. New web/src/analyze/analyzeEntry.ts: AnalyzeIntent enum (memo|compare|general), analyzeEntryFromSubject(subject, intent?) builds canonical /analyze?subject=kind:uuid[&intent=...] URL paired with hydrated ResolvedSubject as React Router state. Strict subject parser rejects malformed input (avoids the lenient parseSubjectRouteParam fallback). SubjectDetailShell now exposes Analyze button next to Save-to-watchlist; AnalyzePage shows carried-subject context block with intent badge or empty entry state. 12 new tests covering URL build, roundtrip, malformed/unknown rejection, state-vs-URL preference, deep-link fallback. 111 web tests green; typecheck + lint + build clean. Verified live in browser: in-app click renders carried subject, top-nav tab renders empty state, deep-link with intent=memo renders subject + intent badge.

All three children landed: fra-cw0.6.1 (holders tab — institutional + insider), fra-cw0.6.2 (signals tab — sentiment_trend + claim_clusters evidence-backed blocks), fra-cw0.6.3 (Analyze entry handoff with carried SubjectRef + intent). Symbol detail now has 5 nested tabs (overview/financials/earnings/holders/signals) plus the explicit Analyze workspace transition. Ready to unblock fra-7vn.2 (P4.2 Analyze template) and fra-7vn.3 (P4.3 shared artifact flow), both of which consume the carried-subject contract.

Closed

Implemented screener executor (filter→sort→page→assemble), saved-screen repository, and HTTP server with all 7 endpoints. 16 new HTTP integration tests + 23 unit tests (executor + candidate registry) + 10 screen-repo tests added; all 115 tests pass. Verification target met: /v1/screener/screens/:id/replay re-runs through the executor on every call (test asserts different as_of stamps across two replays of the same screen). Stacked on PR #26 branch; ready for its own PR once that merges.

Portfolio model with required base_currency: HTTP service at services/portfolio with POST/GET/DELETE endpoints, contract validator that rejects missing or non-ISO 4217 base_currency at the API boundary, integration tests against real Postgres confirming required base_currency. Holdings (.9.2) and overlay inputs (.9.3) remain open under fra-cw0.9.

PortfolioHolding bound to canonical market identity: HOLDING_SUBJECT_KINDS allowlist (instrument, listing) enforced at the API contract boundary in services/portfolio/src/holdings.ts; raw ticker strings rejected via UUID v4 validation on subject_id. Holdings HTTP routes layered onto the existing portfolio service with parent-portfolio ownership check before any holding access. 28 integration tests verify each forbidden subject_kind (theme, macro_topic, portfolio, screen, issuer) returns 400, plus cascade delete and cross-user 404 semantics. Overlay input read model (.9.3) remains open under fra-cw0.9.

Overlay input read model: POST /v1/portfolios/overlays returns one entry per requested subject_ref with per-portfolio contributions (portfolio_id, base_currency, quantity, cost_basis, held_state, opened_at, closed_at). Lockstep unnest($2::text[], $3::uuid[]) join keeps the bulk lookup to one round trip. User-scoped — cross-user holdings never leak. Verification: USD and EUR portfolios holding the same instrument contribute as two distinct entries, no FX netting. 18 tests pass (10 unit, 8 integration). Closes parent fra-cw0.9 (.9.1, .9.2, .9.3 all delivered).

P1.5 delivered: Portfolio model (.9.1) with required base_currency, PortfolioHolding (.9.2) bound to instrument/listing canonical identity (theme/screen/etc rejected), and overlay-input read model (.9.3) keyed by subject + contributing portfolio with no implicit FX netting. Service at services/portfolio with HTTP CRUD plus POST /v1/portfolios/overlays. Downstream consumer fra-cw0.10 (P1.5b first surface behaviors) is now unblocked.

Screener workspace delivered: query controls (universe / market / fundamentals filters, sort, page) and result rows in one /screener surface. Form submit reissues POST /v1/screener/search and re-renders rows in place — URL never leaves /screener (verification bullet met). Row click hands off canonical SubjectRef to symbol-detail via symbolDetailPathForSubject. Saved-screen CRUD (session-gated) is fra-cw0.8.2.

Saved-screen CRUD with session gate delivered. Save / open / delete all flow through the auth interrupt: SaveScreen protected-action variant added in authInterruptState.ts with top-level validator on the ScreenerQuery envelope; an unauthed Save click queues the full {name, definition} payload to sessionStorage and opens the inline modal, and on sign-in the registered handler POSTs to /v1/screener/screens. Verification met end-to-end via Playwright (modal opens with current screen definition preserved, sign-in completes the save, panel populates with the saved screen, Open restores it, Delete removes it). Closes parent fra-cw0.8 contract together with fra-cw0.8.1.

Both-states visibility delivered. Subject-detail header reports watchlisted and held membership separately — a subject that is both renders two distinct badges; the two states are derived independently and never collapse. Watchlist membership lifts into a context provider in WorkspaceShell (rail and header read from one source); held membership comes from per-subject lookup against POST /v1/portfolios/overlays. Pure isSubjectWatchlisted() derivation tested for cross-kind id-collision invariant. Shipped together with fra-cw0.10.1 in the combined PR.
Delivered in PR #34 — narrative + chart/comparison block renderers, BlockRegistry, fixtures, and pure-helper tests
Delivered in PR #34 — narrative + chart/comparison block renderers, BlockRegistry, fixtures, and pure-helper tests
Delivered in PR #34 — agent_run_logs table (migration 0004 + canonical schema mirror) and start/completeAgentRunLog writers in services/observability

Renderers implemented and registered; fixture invariants pass; typecheck/lint/build clean. Ref-resolution verification deferred to data-resolver bead (fra-8cb) and Sources auto-render verification deferred to chat-orchestration.

Renderers implemented and registered; fixture invariants pass; typecheck/lint/build clean. Ref-resolution verification deferred to data-resolver bead (fra-8cb) and Sources auto-render verification deferred to chat-orchestration.

Implemented services/tools registry loader for spec/finance_research_tool_registry.json with validation, query helpers, immutable outputs, and tests covering default/custom registry loading.
Implemented registry-backed bundle selector for pre-resolve bundle_id classifications with graceful unknown-bundle rejection and tests across all registered bundle names.
Implemented services/tools audience enforcement with static registry scans, audience-filtered bundle tools, per-call authorization, and adversarial raw_blob_url rejection tests.

Tightened analyst registry schemas by replacing analyst-side permissive additionalProperties with raw-key-guarded analyst_safe_json_value refs, and extended static audience validation/tests to reject permissive or unguarded analyst schema maps.

Implemented services/tools approval interceptor that routes approval_required tools such as create_alert and create_agent into pending actions with deterministic pending_action_id, while allowing non-approval write-intent tools to execute with write_intent policy metadata.

Resolved in PR #36 review follow-up: writeCitationLogsForBlocks now performs one atomic insert statement for block-derived citation rows, with tests covering single-statement batch writes.
Implemented deterministic golden-eval drift reports comparing consecutive eval_run_results rows, with synthetic regression tests and public exports.
Implemented Source repo helpers with trust_tier/license_class validation, source lookup, package tests, and document-source FK integration coverage.
Implemented idempotent Document repo creation keyed by (content_hash, raw_blob_id), with created/already_present results and double-ingest coverage.

Accepted PX.2 after verifying all tool-runtime child beads are closed, services/tools tests cover registry loading, bundle selection, audience enforcement, budget gating, approval interception, prompt templates, and CI now runs the tools test suite.

Accepted P2.3 after verifying all block-registry child beads are closed and web gates pass: schema validator, canonical data_ref naming, 20 block renderer catalog, RichText ref resolver, fixture coverage, tests, typecheck, and lint.

Accepted PX.1 after verifying all observability child beads are closed and services/observability tests cover tool_call_logs, citation_logs, verifier_fail_logs, eval_run_results, golden evals, drift reports, and agent_run_logs; db tests also pass locally with Docker-only cases skipped.
Implemented snapshot manifest staging from tool-call contributions with first-seen dedupe, metadata validation, and tool_call_logs audit coverage. Verified with services/snapshot npm test.
Implemented deterministic disclosure policy compiler for delayed pricing, filing-time basis, low coverage, candidate data, and FX conversion signals. Verified with services/snapshot npm test.
Implemented deterministic snapshot seal verifier for refs, sources, fact unit/period bindings, disclosures, approvals, and verifier_fail_logs writes. Verified with services/snapshot npm test.

Implemented content-addressed ObjectStore contract in services/evidence: ObjectStore interface (put/get/has, no update/delete), MemoryObjectStore implementation, sha256:<hex> raw_blob_id derivation, defensive byte copying at both ends (immutability invariant). Verified with services/evidence npm test (20 pass: 11 new object-store tests + 9 existing repos). The S3-compatible wire adapter for R2/S3/MinIO is filed as fra-ujp and blocks parent fra-6j0.1.

Implemented document threading traversal in services/evidence: getDocumentChildren (direct replies), getDocumentAncestors (recursive CTE root-first), getDocumentThread (recursive CTE level-order/BFS), getConversation (group by conversation_id). Migration 0007 adds partial indexes on parent_document_id and conversation_id (sparse columns). README documents BFS ordering, cycle prevention via insert order, and the silent-keep-original-parent behavior on re-ingestion conflict. Verified with services/evidence npm test (31 pass: 11 new threading + 20 existing) and db npm test (34 pass: integration tests for new migration up/down). Code review caught BFS-vs-DFS terminology error and silent parent_document_id conflict behavior; both addressed in commit 15544b2 with documentation fixes and a pinning test.

Implemented S3-compatible ObjectStore wire adapter in services/evidence/src/s3-object-store.ts. put HEADs first and short-circuits to already_present on hit; otherwise uploads with ChecksumSHA256 for server-side body verification. get/has translate 404 to null/false; non-404 errors propagate. Keys namespaced as {keyPrefix?}/sha256/{hex} for future hash-family extensibility. First @aws-sdk/client-s3 dependency in services/. .env.dev.example documents BLOB_STORE_BACKEND=memory|s3 plus S3_*; docker-compose.dev.yml gains a MinIO service. Verified with services/evidence npm test (50 pass: 14 unit with mocked S3 client + 4 real-MinIO integration via new bootstrapMinio harness + 32 pre-existing). Code review caught isNotFoundError trusting httpStatusCode too broadly (would mask R2's AccessDenied-as-404), FIFO t.after ordering risk, and MinIO image pinning; all addressed in commit 8056278.

Accepted P3.1 after all four child beads and the S3 wire adapter dependency landed: fra-5gb (Source repo + trust_tier + license_class), fra-131 (Document repo + content-hash dedupe), fra-8la (parent_document_id threading + recursive-CTE traversal helpers), fra-0v4 (content-addressed ObjectStore contract + MemoryObjectStore), fra-ujp (S3ObjectStore wire adapter for R2/S3/MinIO). Schema acceptance: documents reference raw_blob_id as sha256:<64-hex>; sources carry trust_tier and license_class; unique index on (content_hash, raw_blob_id) prevents duplicates (pinned by integration test for the realistic non-conflict case where they differ); parent_document_id supports threaded sources with traversal helpers; deleted_at column present for retention; raw blobs are read-only (ObjectStore has no update/delete; content-addressed). Verified with services/evidence npm test (50 pass: unit + Docker-gated integration via bootstrapMinio + bootstrapDatabase) and db npm test (34 pass). Three rounds of code review (per-bead × 2 + cross-cutting integration) caught and resolved: contract-divergence between document-repo and object-store, BFS-vs-DFS terminology mismatch, parent_document_id silent-keep on conflict, isNotFoundError too-broad 404 fallback (R2 edge case), and FIFO t.after ordering. Branch feat/fra-gfq-source-doc-repos head 8056278.

Implemented /v1/chat/threads CRUD: list (active by default, ?include_archived=true to include archived), POST create, PATCH title, DELETE soft-archive. User-scoped via x-user-id header; cross-user access returns 404 with no existence leak. Soft-delete uses coalesce(archived_at, now()) for retry idempotency. Migration 0008 adds chat_threads.archived_at. Coverage: 19 unit + 16 HTTP + 4 docker-backed integration tests (101 chat tests pass; 34 db tests pass). OpenAPI updated with PATCH/DELETE endpoints, archived_at field, and OAS 3.1-correct nullable types per code review.

SSE reconnect with Last-Event-ID was already implemented; the gap was verification. Added 3 deferred-gated async-runner tests that pin the bead's contract: (1) mid-stream client disconnect doesn't abort the turn — server-side emissions accumulate in coordinator history while no subscriber exists, and reconnect with Last-Event-ID:N replays them strictly in seq>N order; (2) turn keeps running through a disconnect — all 9 events emit server-side even when the client received only #1; (3) reconnect across a wall-clock gap with no in-flight events stays open until the runner produces more. Comments document microtask-drain bounds and the live-vs-history-replay paths per code review. 104 chat tests pass.

The SSE event schema, sequencer, correlation-field enforcement, and the 10-kind event set were all already implemented in services/chat/src/sse.ts. The gap was unit-test coverage of the bead's contract. Added services/chat/test/sse.test.ts with 10 focused tests pinning: (1) every one of the 10 event kinds carries seq + turn_id + thread_id + run_id + type via the sequencer; (2) turn_id defaults to run_id when not provided; (3) sequencer produces strictly monotonic seq across kind transitions; (4) payload fields cannot forge the universal fields (spread order); (5) block.* requires block_id, tool.* requires tool_call_id, snapshot.* requires snapshot_id, all rejecting whitespace-only ids; (6) turn.* requires no extra correlation field; (7) createChatSseEvent rejects 0/negative/fractional/NaN/Infinity for seq; (8) stubSuccessEvents returns the 9-event canonical success stream with turn.error reserved for the failure path. Bead's verification step (Kill+resume SSE; last-seq reconnect completes stream) is satisfied by the combination of these unit tests and fra-cty's sse-resume.test.ts. Per code review, removed the unused initialSeq parameter from createChatSseSequencer to eliminate a future-misuse vector. 114 chat tests pass.

All five children closed; acceptance contract met: per-thread coordinator (fra-eom), SSE event schema + monotonic seq (fra-u9l), Last-Event-ID reconnect (fra-cty), chat_messages persisted only after snapshot sealing (fra-d7t), /v1/chat/threads CRUD (fra-2fu.1.1).

Implemented deterministic quote-only fast-path detector in services/tools/. Conservative regex-based heuristic with analyst-opinion containment, char/token caps, and required exactly-one resolved subject. Returns frozen FastPathDecision carrying classification, detector_version, and heuristic_ms for instrumentation. 10 tests including the canonical 'AAPL price' contract under 100ms and 7 analyst-opinion counterexamples. Reviewed; review feedback addressed (analyst-opinion gap closed, API surface added classification field, heuristic_ms scope documented, dead branch collapsed).
Closed

Implemented createDefaultBlockRegistry() composing all 4 register*Blocks helpers; mounted singleton at App root via BlockRegistryProvider so chat, Analyze, Home, and findings share one registry. Drift-detection test covers schema enum ↔ typed kind groups ↔ register helpers ↔ composer. Web tests 220/220, typecheck and lint clean.

Implemented computeVirtualWindow (pure O(N) windowing math; perf test confirms <500µs/call at 1000 items, well under the 16.67ms/frame budget), VirtualizedMessageList (rAF-batched scroll, ResizeObserver-measured variable-height items, constant rendered count regardless of total length), MemoizedBlockView (React.memo + reference-equality comparator since snapshot blocks are immutable), MessageItem (memoized + content_hash as React key for cross-snapshot identity). Exported via web/src/chat/index.ts. Tests 232/232 (+12), typecheck and lint clean.

Implemented isAtBottom (pure predicate over ScrollPosition with 50px default threshold absorbing subpixel rounding), JumpToLatestButton (absolute-positioned bottom-right), and tailing logic in VirtualizedMessageList (wasAtBottomRef captured on every scroll; useLayoutEffect on messages auto-scrolls only when last-message-id changed AND user was at bottom; jump button visible iff !isAtBottom). Tests 238/238 (+6 scroll-tailing predicate cases: exact bottom, within threshold, past threshold, no-overflow case, custom threshold, threshold-positive sanity). Typecheck and lint clean.

Implemented pure transport-agnostic streamReducer (turn.started/completed/error + block.began/delta/completed → StreamState; rich_text segments accumulate from delta payload, opaque kinds hold skeleton until completed; reference-equality bailout for irrelevant events), useChatStream hook (subscribe-style transport adapter wraps useReducer), StreamingBlockView (skeleton → progressive RichText → completed for rich_text; per-kind skeleton heights for everything else), StreamingTurnView (renders the in-progress turn from StreamState), BlockSkeleton (pulse-animated per-kind heights), CHAT_SSE_EVENT_TYPES frontend mirror with backend drift test. Tests 255/255 (+17): 14 reducer cases (init, turn.started reset, began variants, delta variants/no-ops, completed/no-ops, turn.completed/error, irrelevant-event reference bailout, multi-block sequence) + SSE event type drift + 2 sse type test cases. Typecheck and lint clean.

parseInferredMembershipSpec + computeInferredThemeCandidates + applyInferredThemeMembership shipped. Rationale chain preserved end-to-end; 50 unit tests pass. Live-SQL integration coverage tracked as fra-fp7.

All children shipped: fra-2ju (Theme/ThemeMembership models), fra-95e (bundle routing), fra-vme (inferred membership). Coordinator wired in 2f03e18 — chooseBundleIdForSubjectKind reaches the runner via ChatTurnRunContext.bundleId; SSE consumers see bundle_id on turn.started/turn.completed. 126 chat tests pass.

4 integration tests against real Postgres, covering both signal-source merging, the support-only contradiction-exclusion contract, min_confidence + impact_directions filters, and applyInferredThemeMembership round-trip with rationale persistence and replay idempotency. All pass against postgres:15 via docker-pg.

Closed addThemeMembership race via unique (theme_id, subject_kind, subject_id) + INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING. Replaced single-column theme_memberships indexes with covering ones matching the list ORDER BY. Verified end-to-end with a 10-writer concurrent integration test. Reviewed: ready to merge. AnalyzeTemplate CRUD shipped at services/analyze. Implements create/get/listByUser/update/delete with COALESCE-driven patch + atomic version bump in SQL. 22 unit tests + 3 docker-pg integration tests (full round-trip, ON DELETE CASCADE, JSON-null-vs-SQL-NULL contract). Code review: ready to merge with one fix (docstring/test for the null-patch contract — applied). Commits: 8c5bb8b, 0847f3b.

Bundle execution layer shipped: services/analyze/src/template-runner.ts (persistAnalyzeTemplateRunAfterSnapshotSeal + *WithPool, getRun, listRunsByTemplate). Schema migration 0010 adds analyze_template_runs with template_version pinning, ON DELETE CASCADE from analyze_templates, and FK-without-cascade on snapshot_id (chat_messages convention). Stale — already fixed by 6 commits landed after the bead was filed: 44372ee (swallow 57P01 in connectedClient), aa1f022 (LIFO teardown ordering), c74dfe7 (startup readiness), 7cd1d62 (schema-apply retry), 5d90745 (connection-reset retry), be112b0 (named disconnect codes + .catch), plus 1acbdc4 (LIFO in migrate tests). Bead's original evidence: ~2/55 local + ~9/55 Linux CI failures. Current evidence: 13/13 local resolver runs clean, 4/4 local observability runs clean, 20/20 consecutive CI runs green going back to 2026-04-28. Bead was never closed after the fix shipped.

Shipped license-class-aware ingest primitive: license-policy.ts (fail-closed PERMISSIVE/EPHEMERAL allowlists, throws on unknown), ingest.ts (orchestrator: compute content_hash always, conditionally put to object-store, then createDocument), ephemeral sentinel raw_blob_id format ('ephemeral:<source_uuid>') with strict storage-layer validation. Bead verification ('Ingest with restrictive license; no blob written') covered by both a unit test and an end-to-end integration test against real pg (twitter source + license_class=ephemeral → documents row with sentinel + zero objectStore.put calls). Schema unchanged. evidence: 72/72 green. Real X/Reddit API ingestion clients deferred to parent fra-6j0.2.

SEC EDGAR ingestion adapter shipped: TokenBucketRateLimiter (capped at SEC's 10 req/sec ceiling, default 8 with injectable clock/sleep), filingArchiveUrl/filingIndexUrl (pure URL builders rejecting path-traversal/malformed accession at the chokepoint), SecEdgarClient (User-Agent enforced at construction per Fair Access policy, 429 classified as SecEdgarRateLimitError, other non-2xx as SecEdgarFetchError, fromEnv() reads SEC_EDGAR_USER_AGENT), ingestSecFiling orchestrator (fetch-first fail-closed ordering: a 404 leaves no half-attributed source row; createSource with provider=sec_edgar/trust_tier=primary/license_class=public; ingestDocument via fra-0sa permissive route with provider_doc_id=accession_number for dedupe). 20 unit tests + 1 env-var-gated live AAPL 10-K test (correctly skipped without SEC_EDGAR_USER_AGENT). evidence: 92/92 green. Commit 9bb2c78.

User-scoped uploads shipped end-to-end. Migration 0011_sources_user_id adds nullable sources.user_id (FK to users w/ ON DELETE CASCADE) plus a partial index sources_user_id_idx WHERE user_id IS NOT NULL — documents inherit visibility scope through their existing source_id FK so no parallel column on documents was needed. New 'user_private' permissive license class (storage retention orthogonal to visibility — uploads MUST be stored). New services/evidence/src/user-uploads.ts orchestrator: ingestUserUpload(deps, {userId, bytes, title, ...}) wires createSource + ingestDocument with the canonical upload defaults (provider=user_upload, kind=upload, trust_tier=user, license_class=user_private); listUserUploadDocuments and getUserUploadDocument enforce user-scoping at the SQL layer (WHERE sources.user_id = $N AND sources.kind = 'upload') and return null on miss (existence-oracle defense). Bead verification covered by integration test against real pg: user A uploads, user B's list returns 0, user B's get-by-id returns null even with the right document_id, user A sees their own doc. Plus defensive integration tests for non-upload sources and ON DELETE CASCADE. evidence: 111/111 (8 new unit + 3 new integration). db: 35/35 (migrate test count + rollback assertions updated). PDF parsing/extraction and HTTP multipart endpoint deferred to follow-on beads (parent fra-6j0.2).
